text_blocks.text: In many countries, there is a Gender pay gap in favor of
males in the labor market. Several factors other than
discrimination may contribute to this gap. On average,
women are more likely than men to consider factors other
than pay when looking for work, and may be less willing to
travel or relocate. Thomas Sowell, in his book Knowledge and
Decisions, claims that this difference is due to women not
taking jobs due to marriage or pregnancy, but income studies
show that that does not explain the entire difference. A
U.S. Census's report stated that in US once other factors
are accounted for there is still a difference in earnings
between women and men. The income gap in other countries
ranges from 53% in Botswana to -40% in Bahrain.
